channel.title: Charter Ed Digest
channel.description: News, trends, and practical guidance for charter school leaders and families

item.title: Same School, Two Pathways: The Dual-Format Charter Trend
item.description: More charter schools now offer both a physical campus and a virtual academy. Here's what's driving the trend and what families should look for.

item.title: Micro-Credentials: The Next Edge for Charter Schools
item.description: Micro-credentials are reshaping K-12 career readiness. Here's why charter schools—especially flexible and independent-study models—are poised to lead.

item.title: Why Charter Schools Struggle to Keep Great Teachers
item.description: Charter schools lose teachers at a higher rate than traditional public schools. Here's what the data says is driving it—and which retention strategies actually work.

item.title: What State Charter Advocacy Really Gets You in 2025
item.description: State charter associations delivered real 2025 policy wins: facilities funds, funding equity, zoning relief. Here's what strong advocacy means for your school.

item.title: How Charter Schools Are Closing the College Counseling Gap
item.description: Most public school students share one counselor with 372 peers. Here's how charter schools build dedicated college prep that starts long before senior year.

item.title: When Charter Schools Expand: What Leaders & Families Need to Know
item.description: Charter schools are growing faster than ever. Here's what leaders must assess—and what families should ask—before a school opens a second campus.

item.title: Do Online Charter Schools Feel Isolating? What the Data Shows
item.description: Parents worry virtual charter schools leave kids disconnected. Here's what the research shows—and how the best online charters are solving it.

item.title: The Workforce Gap Charter Schools Are Built to Close
item.description: 84% of hiring managers say high school grads aren't workforce-ready. Here's how charter schools with CTE pathways are changing that.

item.title: Why Charter Schools Are Leading on Environmental Ed
item.description: Environmental stewardship is now a top-10 fastest-growing workforce skill. Here's how mission-driven charter schools are weaving it into K-12 learning.

item.title: Why Charter Enrollment Keeps Growing in 2025
item.description: Charter schools hit a record 4 million students in 2025. Here's what's driving families to choose charters—and what it means for your school.

item.title: Charter School Demand Is Soaring. Buildings Are the Bottleneck.
item.description: Charter enrollment hits nearly 4 million nationwide, but facilities remain the #1 growth barrier. Here's what's changing—and what leaders can do now.

item.title: Why College Readiness Must Start Before High School
item.description: Dual enrollment is booming, but research shows students succeed only when college-prep habits are built early—starting in elementary and middle school.

item.title: The ABA–School Readiness Gap Florida Autism Families Face
item.description: Florida families of young children with autism face a painful choice between ABA therapy and school readiness prep. Here's what's changing—and why it matters.

item.title: How Charter Schools Build a College-Going Culture
item.description: The best charter schools don't just offer college prep classes—they build a whole-school culture around it. Here's what the research says actually works.

item.title: Micro-Credentials Are Coming to K-12—Is Your School Ready?
item.description: Micro-credentials are reshaping K-12 career readiness. Here's what charter school leaders and families need to know about this fast-moving trend.

item.title: Is a Virtual Charter School Right for Your Family?
item.description: Virtual charter school enrollment keeps rising. Here's what the latest data says—and what families should weigh before making the switch.

item.title: Why Knowledge-Rich Curricula Work—and Who's Leading
item.description: New research shows content-rich, history-focused K–6 curricula boost reading scores by 16 points. What charter leaders and families need to know.

item.title: Online Charter Schools Add In-Person Days—Here's Why
item.description: Virtual charter schools are adding regular in-person events to close the socialization gap. Here's what the research shows and how leading schools are doing it.

item.title: The Charter Facilities Gap Is Finally Getting Fixed
item.description: Eight states passed new charter facilities funding laws in 2025. Here's what the landmark changes mean for school leaders, families, and the sector's growth.

item.title: Charter Schools Are Redefining What a Diploma Is Worth
item.description: More charter schools are graduating students with industry credentials alongside diplomas. Here's what the trend means for leaders and families in 2025.
